title: Medical device use in diabetes care - Canada.ca
content:
Diabetes is one of the most common chronic diseases affecting people living in Canada. It occurs when the body doesn't produce enough insulin or can't use the insulin it produces. Diabetes can lead to serious complications, but there are ways to help control the disease and lower the risk of complications. For example, medical devices are used to help measure or monitor blood glucose levels or deliver insulin.
Read more about some types of medical devices used to help manage diabetes.

## Services and information

### Blood glucose monitors
About blood glucose monitors and test strips, what causes inaccurate readings, tips for accurate test results
About insulin pumps and how they work, reducing your risk of problems, Health Canada's role

### Unauthorized glucose monitoring smartwatches
About unauthorized blood glucose reading smartwatches, risks, what you should know

### Continuous  glucose monitor safety alerts on smart devices
